channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What we know about the attack on a synagogue in Michigan
item.description: A man drove a truck into a synagogue in Michigan, according to authorities. The suspected attacker is dead and a guard was injured.
item.content:encoded: A man drove a truck into a synagogue in Michigan, according to authorities. The suspected attacker is dead and a guard was injured.
item.dc:creator: Alex McLenon

item.title: Trump blames Iran for the war — critics question the diplomacy
item.description: Critics say diplomacy with Iran may have been a cover for military buildup, as questions grow over whether the U.S. walked away from a real chance at a deal.
item.content:encoded: Critics say diplomacy with Iran may have been a cover for military buildup, as questions grow over whether the U.S. walked away from a real chance at a deal.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Paralympics star Oksana Masters shares her experience with this year's Games
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Paralympic athlete Oksana Masters about her experiences at this year's Games in Italy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Paralympic athlete Oksana Masters about her experiences at this year's Games in Italy.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Handel

item.title: Paying families of organ donors would save lives, these economists say
item.description: Thousands die every year waiting for an organ donation. The Indicator team talks to two economists who argue that paying the families of organ donors would save lives.
item.content:encoded: Thousands die every year waiting for an organ donation. The Indicator team talks to two economists who argue that paying the families of organ donors would save lives.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Comedian Chris Fleming loves Terry Gross
item.description: In the new HBO special from Chris Fleming, the comedian spends a lot of time talking about Terry Gross. Wild Card host Rachel Martin talks to Fleming about what he loves about the Fresh Air host.
item.content:encoded: In the new HBO special from Chris Fleming, the comedian spends a lot of time talking about Terry Gross. Wild Card host Rachel Martin talks to Fleming about what he loves about the Fresh Air host.

item.title: Exercise rates are still a non-moving target
item.description: Dance teachers, health researchers, urban planners — they all have a desire to get people moving. But globally, exercise rates have remained stagnant.
item.content:encoded: Dance teachers, health researchers, urban planners — they all have a desire to get people moving. But globally, exercise rates have remained stagnant.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
item.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: After firings, funding cuts and a shooting, can a demoralized CDC workforce recover?
item.description: It's been a year since mass firings began at the CDC, the federal public health agency. Then came a shooting, and the government shutdown. Atlanta is still feeling the economic and emotional effects.
item.content:encoded: It's been a year since mass firings began at the CDC, the federal public health agency. Then came a shooting, and the government shutdown. Atlanta is still feeling the economic and emotional effects.
item.dc:creator: Jess Mador

item.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
item.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.
item.content:encoded: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Trump's peace plan for Gaza stalls as Iran war grinds on
item.description: Gains made in Gaza ceasefire slide as Trump's attention turns to Iran war and Israel tightens its siege
item.content:encoded: Gains made in Gaza ceasefire slide as Trump's attention turns to Iran war and Israel tightens its siege
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy
